skill.name: sitemapkit
skill.description: Discover sitemap files and extract URL lists from websites through the SitemapKit MCP server.
skill.summary: Find sitemap files, extract sitemap URLs, and run complete sitemap-based URL discovery.
skill.author: 0nl1n1n

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak crypto, filesystem, network, and credential patterns. Review found the command and weak-crypto alerts are false positives from Markdown backticks, TypeScript template strings, and documentation text, while network access and SITEMAPKIT_API_KEY usage are intentional for the SitemapKit API. The skill is publishable with a warning because user-supplied URLs and an API key are sent to a third-party service.

medium_findings.title: Third-party API receives user URLs and API key
medium_findings.description: The MCP server reads SITEMAPKIT_API_KEY from the environment and sends it with user-provided sitemap or domain URLs to https://app.sitemapkit.com. This is expected for the service, but it exposes browsing targets and credentials to a third-party API and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly reads SITEMAPKIT_API_KEY and sends it in the x-api-key header during fetch calls to the SitemapKit API. This is semantically required for the skill, so the concern is disclosure and user consent, not confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Remote crawling delegated to external service
medium_findings.description: Tool calls accept user-provided url values and forward them to the SitemapKit API for discovery or extraction. This can reveal target domains or sitemap URLs and can consume a user quota on the external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tool handlers forward request arguments to callApi for discover, extract, and full crawl operations. The behavior matches the skill purpose, but users should understand the privacy and quota impact.

low_findings.title: Documentation references hidden MCP configuration files
low_findings.description: README.md mentions user configuration paths such as .cursor/mcp.json and ~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json. These are setup instructions, not code that reads or writes hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only hidden-file references are in documentation lines that tell users where to configure MCP clients. No implementation code accesses those paths.

low_findings.title: Static command-execution alerts are Markdown and string formatting false positives
low_findings.description: The backtick detections in README.md and SKILL.md are inline code formatting, and the detections in src/index.ts are template literals used to format API responses and errors. No shell execution API or dynamic command invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found Markdown code spans, JSON examples, and TypeScript interpolation only. There is no child_process, eval, Function constructor, or shell invocation in the inspected files.

low_findings.title: Weak-crypto alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography locations do not show cryptographic code. They appear to match ordinary text such as Markdown, MCP, sitemap, or example strings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or crypto module usage at the cited locations. The alerts are consistent with substring matches in non-crypto documentation and descriptions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated outbound fetch to fixed API endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: fetch posts JSON bodies to the fixed SitemapKit endpoint with an API key header. This is not malicious by itself, but it is the main security-relevant behavior users must understand before enabling the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The fetch call and x-api-key header are explicit in the implementation. The endpoint is fixed to SitemapKit, reducing exfiltration ambiguity but still requiring disclosure.

content.value_statement: Website URL discovery is slow when sitemap locations are unknown. This skill uses SitemapKit to find sitemap files, extract URLs, and summarize crawl coverage.
actual_capabilities: Finds sitemap files for a supplied website domain.
Extracts URL entries from a direct sitemap URL.
Runs a full sitemap crawl across discovered sitemap files.
Returns URL counts, processed sitemap counts, and truncation status.
Reports remaining SitemapKit quota when the API response includes quota metadata.
Provides setup guidance for Claude Desktop, Cursor, and Windsurf MCP clients.
limitations: Requires a valid SITEMAPKIT_API_KEY environment variable.
Sends target URLs and domains to the SitemapKit API.
Maximum returned URLs depend on the user plan and max_urls setting.
It discovers URLs from sitemaps, not from a full browser crawl of every page.

use_cases.title: SEO site inventory
use_cases.description: Collect sitemap URLs and page counts before checking index coverage or migration readiness.

use_cases.title: Competitive content mapping
use_cases.description: Extract public sitemap URLs from a competitor domain to understand site sections and publishing scale.

use_cases.title: Developer migration checks
use_cases.description: Compare expected pages against sitemap output before or after a site launch.

prompt_templates.title: Find sitemap files
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all sitemap files for https://example.com and tell me where each sitemap was discovered.

prompt_templates.title: Extract one sitemap
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the URLs from https://example.com/sitemap.xml and include last modified dates when available.

prompt_templates.title: Audit site URL count
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full sitemap crawl for https://example.com with a limit of 5000 URLs. Summarize total URLs, processed sitemaps, and truncation status.

prompt_templates.title: Compare launch coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Discover all sitemaps for https://example.com, group the returned URLs by site section, and flag sections with unexpectedly low page counts.

output_examples.output: A concise list of discovered sitemap URLs, the discovery methods used, and remaining monthly quota when available.

output_examples.input: Extract every URL from a product sitemap.
output_examples.output: A readable URL list with optional last modified dates and a note if the result was truncated.

output_examples.input: Run a full crawl for a website before migration.
output_examples.output: A summary of sitemap counts, processed sitemap files, total URLs, truncation status, and representative URL sections.

best_practices: Confirm the target domain is public and appropriate to send to SitemapKit.
Start with sitemap discovery before running a full crawl on large sites.
Set max_urls deliberately to control result size and quota usage.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for private URLs or sensitive internal domains.
Do not assume a sitemap contains every live page on a site.
Do not request very high URL limits without checking quota and truncation status.

faq.question: Does this skill need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The MCP server requires SITEMAPKIT_API_KEY to authenticate with SitemapKit.

faq.question: Does it send data outside my environment?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends the target domain or sitemap URL to the SitemapKit API.

faq.question: Can it crawl pages without a sitemap?
faq.answer: It focuses on sitemap discovery and extraction. It is not a full browser crawler.

faq.question: What happens when results are too large?
faq.answer: The response can be truncated. Increase max_urls if your plan and quota allow it.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through MCP-compatible setup.

faq.question: Can it show remaining quota?
faq.answer: Yes. It reports remaining monthly requests when SitemapKit returns quota metadata.
